description: Describes a single metric including its metadata, supported aggregations, entity dimensions, and default aggregation settings.
name: MetricDescriptor

metricId.description: The fully qualified key of the metric, potentially including applied transformations. For example, builtin:host.cpu.usage:avg.

displayName.description: The human-readable display name of the metric.

description.description: A detailed description of what the metric measures.

unit.description: The unit of the metric values. For example, Percent, Byte, MilliSecond.

dduBillable.description: Whether ingesting this metric consumes Davis Data Units (DDUs).

created.description: The Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the metric was created.

lastWritten.description: The Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the metric was last written.

entityType.description: The entity types that can be used to filter this metric, e.g., HOST, SERVICE, PROCESS_GROUP.

aggregationTypes.description: The list of aggregation types supported for this metric. Valid values include min, max, sum, count, avg, median, and percentile.

dimensionDefinitions.description: The list of dimension definitions for this metric. Each dimension provides a label for data splitting.

items.description: Defines a single dimension of a metric.

key.description: The unique key identifying this dimension.

name.description: The display name of the dimension.

type.description: The type of the dimension. For example, ENTITY for entity dimensions or STRING for string dimensions.

displayName.description: The human-readable display name of the dimension.

transformations.description: The list of transformation expressions that can be applied to this metric using the metric selector.

defaultAggregation.description: The default aggregation setting for the metric.

type.description: The aggregation type to apply when no aggregation is specified in the query. For example, avg, sum, or max.

parameter.description: The parameter for the aggregation type, used for percentile aggregation to specify the percentile value (e.g., 95).
